description: This figure displays the mechanical property evolution of an Fe-Mn-Al austenitic alloy with nominal composition 27.5 wt% Mn, 9.1 wt% Al, and 0.92 wt% C as a function of 16 h isothermal aging temperature. The x-axis is aging temperature in °C, the y-axis reports two types of parameters: ultimate tensile strength (σ_b) and 0.2% offset yield strength (σ_0.2) in kg/mm², plus elongation (δ) and reduction in area (ψ) in %. Both σ_b and σ_0.2 increase continuously with aging temperature up to 550 °C, peaking at ~95 kg/mm² and ~85 kg/mm² respectively, then decrease slightly above 550 °C. Conversely, δ and ψ decrease with increasing aging temperature to 550 °C, reaching minima of ~10% and ~10% respectively, before partially recovering at temperatures above 550 °C. The figure supports the conclusion that peak strength occurs at 550 °C from dispersed phase precipitation, while aging above 550 °C reduces strength and restores plasticity via precipitate coalescence.
